rationale.functionality: All 9 acceptance criteria verified. (1) Rules 7/8/9 in scripts/hooks/enforce-leader-worktree.sh lines 70-83 are byte-for-byte identical to git show f0410d7^:scripts/hooks/enforce-leader-worktree.sh lines 70-82 (verified via diff — clean match). (2) Error message line 92 updated to list all 9 categories: '.selfmodel/、.claude/、scripts/、playbook/、*.md、.gitignore、LICENSE/VERSION/CHANGELOG、.github/、assets/'. (3) scripts/selfmodel.sh heredoc (lines 1720-1734) mirrors the same Rules 7/8/9 with identical patterns and the same updated error message. (4) New scripts/tests/test-hook-drift.sh exists (101 lines, 0755). (5) chmod +x verified (-rwxr-xr-x). (6) Ran the drift test in the worktree with BYPASS_LEADER_RULES unset → exit 0 + '✅ hook file matches canonical heredoc'. (7-9) Smoke test in worktree (BYPASS unset): VERSION/LICENSE/CHANGELOG.md/.github/workflows/ci.yml/assets/logo.svg all exit 0; src/main.ts exits 2 with the 🚨 banner on stderr. Drift mutation test: appended a stray '# DRIFT' to live hook → drift test correctly returns exit 1 with full diff output, proving it actually catches drift, not just rubber-stamps the current state.
rationale.code_quality: set -euo pipefail at the top of both shell additions. No TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/mock/placeholder anywhere in the diff. The new test uses proper trap-based tmpdir cleanup ('trap rm -rf ${TMP_DIR} EXIT'), distinct exit codes (0=clean, 1=drift, 2=test infra failure), and routes errors to stderr. awk-based heredoc extraction is robust to surrounding context (anchored on the exact 'cat > .../enforce-leader-worktree.sh << HOOKEOF' sentinel). Pre-flight checks confirm both source files exist before extraction, and an empty extraction is treated as exit 2 with a descriptive sentinel hint. No AI slop: comments are dense and load-bearing (background, intent, exit-code spec, fix instructions), no flattery, no over-explanation of obvious code, no synonym soup, no template catch blocks. Bilingual Chinese+English comments match selfmodel project style.
rationale.design_taste: Naming reads like prose: LIVE_HOOK / CANONICAL_SOURCE / EXTRACTED / REPO_ROOT / TMP_DIR — every variable's purpose is obvious at the call site. The drift failure message gives operators the three-step recovery procedure (decide intent, sync the other side, rerun) instead of just dumping a diff. Exit-code triad (0/1/2) cleanly separates 'clean', 'drift', and 'test broken' so CI dashboards can distinguish regression from infra rot. Comment header explicitly references the offending commit f0410d7 and the audit doc, so future readers know exactly what this test exists to prevent.
rationale.completeness: Both error branches in the drift test are handled (missing live hook, missing canonical source, failed extraction). diff is captured to a file before being printed so the success path doesn't pollute stdout. The trap is registered before any temp files are written, so cleanup is unconditional. Smoke test in the contract is fully covered. The only gap I can find: the test only guards enforce-leader-worktree.sh, not the other three hooks (enforce-dispatch-gate, enforce-depth-gate, enforce-agent-rules) which are generated by the same generate_hooks() function and have the same drift risk class. The contract scope is limited to leader-worktree, so this is a should_fix follow-up Sprint, not a blocker for AC pass.
rationale.integration_depth: Restored Rules 7/8/9 use the exact same 'comment header + if [[ ${NORMALIZED} == pattern ]]; then exit 0; fi' template as Rules 1-6 — same indentation, same fi placement, same blank-line spacing, same English comment style. The selfmodel.sh heredoc edit lands at exactly the structural twin location, so generate_hooks() now writes a byte-identical file (verified by the test itself). The new test lives under scripts/tests/ following the existing repo convention. SCRIPT_DIR/REPO_ROOT bootstrap idiom matches what the rest of scripts/hooks/ uses. The drift test directly closes the regression class that produced this Sprint — it's not a tangential fix, it's the structural lock that prevents R4 from happening again. Settings.json untouched (correct — hook is registered already). Other hooks untouched (correctly out of scope).
rationale.originality: The awk single-pass extraction with the exact-string sentinel match is the right tool for the job — sed would need escaping gymnastics, grep can't capture a range cleanly, and a Python script would be heavier than the hook itself. Using diff -u to a temp file means the success path is silent and the failure path gets a precise unified diff for free. The single-quoted heredoc invariant ('HOOKEOF') is explicitly called out in a comment so future maintainers know why awk extraction is safe (no shell expansion to undo). Could have been even more elegant by reusing a generate_hook_to_stdout helper from selfmodel.sh, but that would require refactoring selfmodel.sh — out of scope and would have ballooned the diff.

should_fix: Generalize test-hook-drift.sh into a loop over all four generated hooks (enforce-leader-worktree, enforce-dispatch-gate, enforce-depth-gate, enforce-agent-rules) so the same drift class is locked down for every hook generated by generate_hooks() — file a follow-up Sprint, do not block this one.
Wire scripts/tests/test-hook-drift.sh into the orchestration loop's post-merge smoke test (or a Stop hook) so drift is caught at merge time, not just when a human remembers to run the test.
praise: Drift mutation test confirmed: appending a stray comment to the live hook causes the test to exit 1 with a precise unified diff — this is a real regression guard, not a tautology that always passes.
Restored Rules 7/8/9 are byte-for-byte identical to the pre-R4 version (verified against git show f0410d7^:scripts/hooks/enforce-leader-worktree.sh lines 70-82).
Both writers (live hook + canonical heredoc in selfmodel.sh) updated in lockstep — the next 'selfmodel update' will produce the corrected file instead of replaying the regression.
Error-path documentation in the drift test gives operators a concrete three-step recovery, not just a stack trace.
Exit-code triad (0/1/2) cleanly separates clean / drifted / test-broken states for CI consumption.
